forecaster_note: The dead-south swell has dropped and shortened overnight — 8.5ft @ 8s from 179°, running ~690kJ. Still dead-S, so the corners keep doing the work: Dee Why and Manly are the clean picks under an early SW offshore, while Long Reef and Curl Curl cop it cross-shore. The wind is the clock today — SW ~14km/h at dawn freshening to a 29km/h southerly by lunch. Friday collapses to a weak 50–80kJ mix and Saturday stays small, but SUNDAY is the one to circle: a fresh ENE pulse (6.8ft @ 10s from 78°) rebuilds energy to ~520–560kJ with a light SW offshore — right in the favoured NE–ENE window that actually feeds Dee Why. — Surf Guru, read from Surfline model data (no written report scraped this run).

wind.summary: SW–SSW offshore early (~14–20 km/h, gusting 34) grooming the corners; freshens and swings more S by lunch (~29 km/h, cross-shore) — the morning is the window.

shark.summary: Shark observed by SLS drone at North Curl Curl Beach 8:56am yesterday (Wed 15 Jul) — inside the last 24h. No detections at Dee Why, Long Reef or Manly this run. Check the live map before you paddle out.

days.label: Today
days.note: The S swell has dropped and shortened — 8.5ft @ 8s from 179°, ~690kJ. Dead-S again, so the corners do the work: Dee Why is a fair 2–3ft under the early SW offshore. The wind is the clock — it freshens to a 29km/h southerly by lunch, so today earlier beats your usual 10–12 slot. Ride the 9:42am high into the drop. 🦈 Drone shark sighting at North Curl Curl yesterday morning — heads-up if you surf there.

swells.label: Dead-S, short-period — wraps into the corner smaller but punchy

dee-why.window: Best early-to-mid morning: the SW offshore holds till ~10am, then the southerly builds to 29km/h cross-shore by lunch. Surf the 9:42am high into the dropping tide.

tip.label: Frontside rights
tip.text: 687kJ at 8s is punchy but shifty — the dead-S wrap still stands walls up off the Point for your frontside. Get in before the wind swings; as the tide drops off the 9:42am high the inside bank starts running.

swells.label: Dead-S, short-period — reef takes it head-on, bumpy faces

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — 3–4ft off the S-facing reef, but the SSW sits cross-shore here and goes onshore by lunch. Early or not at all; size over shape.

tip.label: Backside lefts, early only
tip.text: Full 687kJ hits the reef head-on but 8s period means walls section fast. Backside here — pick the sets that stand up on the outside ledge and don't fight the mid-morning bump.

swells.label: Dead-S, short-period — S-magnet loads up but cross-shore texture

curl-curl.window: 2–3ft with cross-shore SSW texture — sectiony rather than clean. A drone spotted a shark here 8:56am yesterday; check the live map and surf with company if you go.
alerts: 🦈 Shark observed by SLS drone at North Curl Curl 8:56am Wed 15 Jul — within the last 24h

tip.label: Shark-aware
tip.text: Conditions are the weakest excuse to roll the dice — same 687kJ as everywhere but bumpier, and yesterday's drone sighting was here. Dee Why or Manly give you the same swell, cleaner, without the asterisk.

swells.label: Dead-S, short-period — north-end shelter trims and tidies it

manly.window: The one spot where the wind stays offshore ALL day — 1–3ft and tidy at North Steyne. If you can only surf late morning after the southerly fills in, this is the move.

tip.label: Late-window insurance
tip.text: Everyone else's clean window closes ~10am; Manly's doesn't. Smaller and softer than Dee Why but groomed — everyday board, work the North Steyne banks off the dropping tide.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: The swell collapses to a weak mix — ~50–80kJ of soft, long-period S lines (1.2ft @ 17s) over small E windswell. Light SSE breeze all day (7–15km/h), nothing stronger. Grovel territory: Dee Why or Manly banks mid-morning if you need a paddle, otherwise bank the energy for Sunday's ENE pulse.

dee-why.window: Soft waist-to-chest peelers on ~50kJ — groveller weather. Mid-morning on the banks is fine if you just want wave count; nothing serious out there.

long-reef.window: Weak and lightly textured on the reef — the long paddle isn't worth 50kJ. Skip.

curl-curl.window: Small, soft and light onshore — low reward even by Friday standards.

manly.window: Waist-high and mellow — fun on the groveller or a log at best, and only just.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Saturday stays small and soft — 1–3ft, ~60–140kJ, light southerly. SUNDAY is the day of the week: a fresh ENE pulse (6.8ft @ 10s from 78°) rebuilds energy to ~520–560kJ with only a light ~7km/h SW offshore. That 78° angle sits right in your favoured NE–ENE window and finally feeds Dee Why the way this week's dead-S never did. Values below are Sunday's — plan the session there, mid-morning.

dee-why.window: Sunday mid-morning is your session of the week — ENE 78° energy, ~520kJ, light SW offshore glass. Punchy, well-fed banks and frontside walls. Saturday isn't worth the wax.

long-reef.window: Picks up the most of the Sunday ENE — 3–4ft (sets to 5ft) with only light wind. Solid open-ocean walls if you want size over polish.

curl-curl.window: Punchy head-high-plus Sunday peaks on the ENE — a real option if Dee Why's carpark is full.

manly.window: Clean 2–3ft Sunday with the light SW offshore — the tidy, sheltered backup to Dee Why.
